objective: Harden fork-local serverless runtime defaults for large-context models by preventing unsafe auto-derived batching values and forcing safer chunked prefill behavior unless explicitly overridden.
scope: Apply minimal, reversible, fork-local runtime/config updates in src/engine_args.py, .runpod/hub.json, and aiwf.request.json only: cap auto-derived max_num_batched_tokens via SAFE_MAX_NUM_BATCHED_TOKENS_CAP, enforce chunked prefill safety for very large contexts with an explicit escape hatch, and tune hub advanced defaults for safer out-of-box behavior.
acceptance_criteria: src/engine_args.py caps auto-derived max_num_batched_tokens using SAFE_MAX_NUM_BATCHED_TOKENS_CAP (default 8192) so large model max lengths do not become unsafe batch-token defaults.
src/engine_args.py forces enable_chunked_prefill=True when max_model_len exceeds 32768 and chunked prefill is disabled, unless ALLOW_UNSAFE_DISABLE_CHUNKED_PREFILL=true is explicitly set.
src/engine_args.py emits concise logs when runtime safety overrides are applied.
.runpod/hub.json sets ENABLE_CHUNKED_PREFILL default to true and lowers MAX_NUM_SEQS default to 16 for safer serverless behavior.
.runpod/hub.json includes advanced SAFE_MAX_NUM_BATCHED_TOKENS_CAP env input with default 8192 aligned to code.
All edits remain fork-local, minimal, and reversible via documented rollback actions.
tests_expected: Run a fork-local RunPod smoke test with a large-context model (e.g., Qwen 3.5 27B) to verify startup stability and no crash from unsafe batching defaults
rollback_plan: Immediate rollback in this fork: remove SAFE_MAX_NUM_BATCHED_TOKENS_CAP logic and large-context chunked-prefill enforcement from src/engine_args.py, restore .runpod/hub.json defaults (ENABLE_CHUNKED_PREFILL=false, MAX_NUM_SEQS=256, remove SAFE_MAX_NUM_BATCHED_TOKENS_CAP entry), and revert this exact three-file change set in one commit. Operational fallback without code rollback: set SAFE_MAX_NUM_BATCHED_TOKENS_CAP to prior desired value and, if needed for troubleshooting only, set ALLOW_UNSAFE_DISABLE_CHUNKED_PREFILL=true. No upstream operations required.
deploy_plan: Validate in this fork only: run py_compile and JSON checks, inspect focused git diff, then run a fork-local RunPod smoke test on a long-context model to confirm the worker starts and serves without OOM/crash from auto-derived batching defaults.
threat_model: Primary risks are throughput regression from tighter defaults, behavior changes for users who previously relied on disabling chunked prefill at high context lengths, and misconfiguration of the new safety cap env. Mitigations: conservative default cap (8192), explicit escape hatch for unsafe disable, concise runtime logs for overrides, and single-commit rollback across only three files.
